applies_to:
  stack: all
  serverless: all

% This file is automatically generated. Don't edit it manually!

# Entity Reference fields [ecs-entity_reference]

A reusable set of identifier fields used to reference other entities in relationship contexts. Each field holds a keyword array of identifiers following the same conventions as the corresponding ECS root field. Only the fields defined in this field set might appear, ad-hoc or integration-specific property names are not allowed.

::::{warning}
This field set is alpha and subject to change.
::::

## Entity Reference field details [_entity_reference_field_details]

| Field | Description | Level |
| --- | --- | --- |
| $$$field-entity-reference-entity-id$$$ [entity_reference.entity.id](#field-entity-reference-entity-id) | Identifiers of referenced entities, using the same meaning as root `entity.id` (stable id for correlation within scope).<br><br>type: keyword<br><br>Note: This field should contain an array of values. | extended |
| $$$field-entity-reference-host-id$$$ [entity_reference.host.id](#field-entity-reference-host-id) | Referenced host ids.<br><br>type: keyword<br><br>Note: This field should contain an array of values. | extended |
| $$$field-entity-reference-host-name$$$ [entity_reference.host.name](#field-entity-reference-host-name) | Referenced host names.<br><br>type: keyword<br><br>Note: This field should contain an array of values. | extended |
| $$$field-entity-reference-service-id$$$ [entity_reference.service.id](#field-entity-reference-service-id) | Referenced service ids.<br><br>type: keyword<br><br>Note: This field should contain an array of values. | extended |
| $$$field-entity-reference-service-name$$$ [entity_reference.service.name](#field-entity-reference-service-name) | Referenced service names.<br><br>type: keyword<br><br>Note: This field should contain an array of values. | extended |
| $$$field-entity-reference-user-domain$$$ [entity_reference.user.domain](#field-entity-reference-user-domain) | Referenced user directory or AD/LDAP domain names (same semantics as ECS `user.domain`).<br><br>type: keyword<br><br>Note: This field should contain an array of values. | extended |
| $$$field-entity-reference-user-email$$$ [entity_reference.user.email](#field-entity-reference-user-email) | Referenced user email addresses.<br><br>type: keyword<br><br>Note: This field should contain an array of values. | extended |
| $$$field-entity-reference-user-id$$$ [entity_reference.user.id](#field-entity-reference-user-id) | Referenced user ids.<br><br>type: keyword<br><br>Note: This field should contain an array of values. | extended |
| $$$field-entity-reference-user-name$$$ [entity_reference.user.name](#field-entity-reference-user-name) | Referenced user short names or logins.<br><br>type: keyword<br><br>Note: This field should contain an array of values. | extended |

## Field reuse [_field_reuse]

The `entity_reference` fields are expected to be nested at:

* `entity.relationships.administers`
* `entity.relationships.depends_on`
* `entity.relationships.owns`
* `entity.relationships.supervises`

Note also that the `entity_reference` fields are not expected to be used directly at the root of the events.
